items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: autolyse in bread making.
items.a: resting flour and water before kneading to improve dough development
items.explanation: Core explanation: resting flour and water before kneading to improve dough development.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: proofing yeast dough.
items.a: allowing dough to rise before baking
items.explanation: Core explanation: allowing dough to rise before baking.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: what curing salt does in preserved meats.
items.a: helps inhibit pathogens and maintain color
items.explanation: Core explanation: helps inhibit pathogens and maintain color.

items.q: Maillard browning is identical to caramelization in all cases.
items.explanation: This aligns with common culinary science understanding.

items.q: Retrogradation contributes to staling in bread products.
items.explanation: This aligns with common culinary science understanding.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: what split peas and lentils share.
items.a: they are pulses high in protein and fiber

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: why chill pastry dough before baking.
items.a: reduces shrinkage and keeps layers tender
items.explanation: Core explanation: reduces shrinkage and keeps layers tender.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: retrogradation in bread.
items.a: starch recrystallization that contributes to staling
items.explanation: Core explanation: starch recrystallization that contributes to staling.

items.q: In food science, what best defines 'emulsion breaking in sauces'?
options: stabilize cocoa butter crystals for gloss and snap
concentrate flavor and thicken via evaporation
items.explanation: This concept is defined as: fat and water phases separate.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: clarified butter definition.
items.a: butterfat with milk solids and water removed
items.explanation: Core explanation: butterfat with milk solids and water removed.
